name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June). **THE FAST FERTILIZER LEG IS PRICING THE PEACE-DIVIDEND HARD — MIDDLE EAST UREA ~$416.50/T (-5.9% 1D), FAR OFF THE ~$935-959/T INDIA PAID IN ITS APRIL TENDERS.** Middle East urea fell ~5.9% in a session to ~$416.50/T as the fertilizer leg prices the de-escalation/diplomacy track (oil down on diplomacy; the 5-6 June Gulf exchange was zero-casualty/calibrated). This is the FAST leg of the physical stack de-pricing on deal-anticipation: the war-premium that drove India's April DAP/urea tenders to ~$935-959/T cfr is bleeding out of the spot complex while the slow legs (insurance ~8x, freight, Qatar Ras Laffan scarring) lag. The mechanism is symmetric and violent: ~65-70% crisis (relief direction) — the same -5.9% reverses UP fast if the talks break (an Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike re-bids urea, cascade path 2: Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security). NOTE: the 5 June broad risk-off (gold -3.1%, oil -2%, VIX +39.7%) was a RATES/AI move (Broadcom miss + hot May jobs), NOT a war risk-off — urea's de-pricing here is the diplomacy/peace track, a distinct driver. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE FERTILIZER WAR-PREMIUM IS DEFLATING MoM — UREA ~$442.50/mt (-23.4% MoM, still +23.8% YoY); CF +0.8%. CONSISTENT WITH GULF DE-ESCALATION, BUT THE STRUCTURAL FLOOR HOLDS.** Urea ~$442.50/mt (+0.6% 1D, -23.4% MoM, +23.8% YoY) — the war-premium is unwinding on the MoM axis as Gulf de-escalation + deal-anticipation price in nitrogen-export normalization, but it remains elevated YoY and India is still paying ~$935-959/t on its April tenders (~2x pre-war). CF Industries $117.52 (+0.8%), Nutrien soft — the equity leg consolidating with the physical de-pricing. The structural floor is intact and slow to unwind: Ras Laffan/QAFCO scarring is years-out, and India's import bill binds through the monsoon planting season regardless of a Hormuz deal. This is the FAST (physical urea / CF equity) leg de-pricing while the slow legs lag — consistent with the broad pattern that re-escalation re-bids it fast (cascade path 2: Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security). PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **THE FERTILIZER WAR-PREMIUM RE-BID — UREA TIGHTNESS REASSERTED AND THE CASCADE-EQUITY LEG (CF +2.5%) FIRED ON A DAY THE BROAD METALS COMPLEX SOLD OFF.** The late-May 'war-premium cracking' read reversed this window: QatarEnergy halted downstream urea when it cut LNG, nitrogen supply tightened, and prices re-bid — urea >$850/mt (April peak, +80% since Feb), US Gulf ~$683/ton (from $516), NOLA +32% in a week. CF Industries +2.5% to $116.31 (Nutrien +0.9%) is the equity manifestation, and it bid UP precisely on a day the entire metals/commodity complex sold off — a clean crisis-linked signal isolating the Qatar-urea cascade (markets E3). This is cascade path 2 (Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security) firing in an attributable way; the fertilizer-urea -> cf-industries edge is strengthened (activation incremented). Reversibility LOW: Ras Laffan/QAFCO scarring is structural; India's import bill binds through the monsoon planting season. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May data). **THE FERTILIZER WAR-PREMIUM IS CRACKING — UREA -35% MoM; THE 'PHYSICAL STACK NOT UNWINDING' THESIS IS NO LONGER UNIFORMLY TRUE. Urea fell to ~$410-443/t (Argus Mid-East granular high-$410s; TradingEconomics generic -35% MoM), and the nitrogen complex de-priced the Qatar-disruption premium hard: CF Industries -11% MoM (-3.6% 1D), Nutrien -7.5% MoM. The chain: deal-anticipation + Modi's fertilizer-cut/import-restraint appeal -> Gulf urea export-normalization expectation -> the fertilizer premium unwinds. **But the bifurcation is now WITHIN the physical complex, not just paper-vs-physical: the FAST legs (fertilizer, oil, LNG equity) de-price first; the SLOW legs (war-risk insurance ~8x, VLCC volumes -36%) lag 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal and have NOT unwound.** Whether shipping/insurance follow is the key tell for the next dossier.** Day 89 (Wednesday May 27 — ~48h since the May 25 brief). **FERTILIZER-EQUITY CONSOLIDATION OFF HIGHS vs PHYSICAL INTACT — CF INDUSTRIES -2.1% 1D / -5.3% MoM & NUTRIEN -4.4% MoM, BUT PHYSICAL UREA >$700/t (+40%, STRUCTURAL; NDSU SEES AN $850-900 PATH). PAPER UNWIND, PHYSICAL INTACT (C6).** The fertilizer-EQUITY leg of the paper crisis-trade unwind: CF/Nutrien consolidated off their highs on deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation (the US strikes May 25 went unanswered) — the same unwind hitting tankers (FRO -3.0%), LNG (Cheniere -10.3% MoM), and defense (LMT -18.8% 3M). But the PHYSICAL fertilizer stack is intact: granular urea >$700/t (+40% from <$500 pre-war; NDSU sees an $850-900 path), DAP elevated, TTF gas feedstock +30% YoY, Qatar Ras Laffan QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients still curtailed (~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected). Paper equities are selling the deal while the physical urea price prices zero operational change. C6 mechanism: even on a clean signed deal the physical fertilizer cost stack (insurance, shipping, gas feedstock) bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session. **Reversibility: the equity component reverses on a deal break (Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike — CF would snap +5-7%, urea re-bids); physical Qatar/Ras Laffan recovery is years out regardless.** PRIOR (May 25): **UREA (GRANULAR FOB ME) ~$502/t, -7.4% 1D — MODERATING ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION, BUT STILL ELEVATED vs ~$300/t PRE-WAR. This is the fertilizer leg of the deal-anticipation tape (paper prices the deal): urea fell with Brent (sub-$100), TTF (-4.2%), as US-Iran deal progress prices a partial Hormuz reopen. The -7.4% is tail-premium de-pricing, NOT a structural reset — the physical cost stack is intact: Qatar Ras Laffan QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients still curtailed, ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected, DAP (FOB Egypt) ~$915-917/t, TTF feedstock still +25% YoY. C4 paper-physical non-confirmation: even on a clean signed deal, the physical fertilizer cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks (insurance, shipping, gas feedstock), not in a session — urea at ~$500 vs ~$300 pre-war is still a ~67% structural premium. CF Industries / Nutrien equities FROZEN at Friday closes (US holiday — NYSE did not settle; do NOT read a Monday move). NOTE (staleness correction): the prior May 19 lead emphasised a CF war-bid on a Brent-up day; today's data is the opposite leg — urea is MODERATING on deal-relief, but the elevated-vs-pre-war structural floor is the durable read. Reversibility: paper component reverses on deal-failure / Israeli kinetic action (CF would snap +5-7%, urea re-bids); physical Qatar/Ras Laffan recovery is years out regardless.** PRIOR (May 19): Day 80 of Hormuz blockade (Monday). **CF INDUSTRIES +2.3% MONDAY TO $125.24 (+0.8% 1M, +34.1% 3M); UREA (YUZHNY/MIDEAST) ~$577/T (+0.8% vs Friday)**. Markets dossier: 33% global fertilizer supply chain contraction; 22M t Gulf urea exports halted (CRU/Global Agriculture); 46% global urea sourced from Gulf; -19% 1M reflects pre-cycle high mean-reversion. **Wheat +2.9% 1D / +10.6% 1M (Black Sea overhang + fertilizer pass-through + Indian export-restriction signals); Corn +2.6% 1D / +4.2% 1M (Black Sea + Trump-Xi trade-not-war + fertilizer pass-through); Rough Rice +1.3% 1D / +16% 1M (India export-restriction momentum); Soybeans +1.7% 1D; Palm Oil +1.2% 1D / +9.7% 1M; Sugar -1.3% 1D**. Iran war fertilizer cascade has knocked out ~49% of global urea export capacity and 30% of ammonia (carries forward). **Nutrien $71.56 (+0.9% 1D)**; Linde $506.11 (-1.1% 1D); BASF €52.63 (-1.4% 1D). **The CF +2.3% Monday is a partial rebound from May 13-15 unwind**: bid back as Brent gaps to $111 + Hormuz tail-risk re-prices in on weekend kinetic tape (UAE Barakah + Saudi drone + Trump-Netanyahu + 'clock is ticking'). **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: Tuesday strike orders → CF +5-7% snap (Hormuz tail-risk re-prices fully); Tuesday back-off → CF retraces toward $115 (Trump-Xi peace-pricing wins); Tuesday nothing → CF oscillates $122-128 with momentum bid intact. Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes still curtailed; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected; structural floor higher. **PRIOR (May 15): Day 77 of Hormuz blockade. C6 CORN +3.7% 1D — WASDE FOLLOW-THROUGH + WHEAT LIMIT-UP SPILLOVER + TRUMP-XI SOYBEAN HALO**. **Urea (Middle East FOB) $562.50/mt May 14 (+1.81% 1D)** (TradingEconomics / Argus reference). Urea Egypt FOB context ~$700/mt (+30% in past month) from $400-490 baseline. **C6 fertilizer cascade unchanged**: Iran war fertilizer cascade has knocked out ~49% of global urea export capacity and 30% of ammonia; Fertilizer Institute: US farmers short ~2 mmt of urea this spring; lower N application → yield risk priced into corn futures. Wheat 1M +9.1% partly drought + Russia tightening; Rough Rice 1M +13.8% anomalous given India lifted export ban = Asian buyer hedging on fertilizer cascade. **CF Industries $122.97 (-2.0% 1D, +1.8% 1M, +31.1% 3M) = FERTILIZER-TRADE UNWIND on Trump-Xi (despite Qatar urea still halted) — ~80% crisis-trade-unwind**. **Nutrien $70.94 (-0.5% 1D, -3.3% 1M, +2.3% 3M)** confirms broader fertilizer sector pressure. **Bessent immediately walked back the soybean piece**: 'soybeans are all taken care of' under existing October 2025 commitment (25 mmt/year through 2028) — hence soybeans only +1.6% not typical 3-5% China-deal pop; corn benefited from optionality without the walk-back ceiling. **Reversibility**: Asymmetric. Reversible legs (WASDE-driven fund longs already crowded; Trump-Xi optimism). Sticky legs (fertilizer-cost-into-yield won't resolve until Hormuz reopens; HRW drought damage). Bias: near-term pullback risk on positioning; structural floor higher as long as Iran war continues. Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. World Bank 'fertilizer prices >+30% 2026' forecast carries forward. **Modi UAE LPG/SPR MoUs May 15 + Fujairah expansion plan publicly disclosed**: India's first-best solution is bilateral Gulf supply — exactly the situation that keeps CF/Nutrien/BASF pricing power despite today's unwind. **C3 CF INDUSTRIES $125.50 (-3.8% 1D, +5.5% 1M) — REVERSAL Day 1, FERTILIZER-TRADE UNWIND**. Drivers (~80% crisis-trade-unwind): (1) PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to **cut fertilizer use 50%** = direct demand-shock signal for India's largest nitrogen import source; (2) Trump-Xi 'trade not war' implies Gulf urea/DAP supply normalization priced in; (3) Q1 EPS beat ($3.98 vs forecast +59%) already priced; stock fell 4.79% post-earnings (Apr) anyway. **Nutrien $71.29 (-1.0% 1D, -3.2% 1M, +2.8% 3M)** confirms broader fertilizer sector pressure but CF is cleaner Gulf-Iran-fertilizer cascade play. **CF +5.5% 1M / -3.8% 1D shows 2-session momentum compounding (May 12-13 +12.9%) IS REVERSING on Trump-Xi summit narrative.** Per markets dossier H reversibility map: CF $110-115 (-8-12% further) if MOU signs by May 21; **CF $145-160 (+15-27% snap-back) if Hormuz remains closed through June — Modi's fertilizer appeal needs actual behaviour change to validate demand-destruction thesis; if Indian wheat-sowing season demand persists, CF reverses upward**. **Urea (Middle East FOB) $552.50/mt May 12 (+0.91% d/d)** (TradingEconomics / Argus reference; up from January spike +$60/mt). Urea Granular FOB ME (futures) $765.00/mt = CME front-month, premium to spot reflects forward supply tightness. **CF/Nutrien equity weakness today is unwinding the FORWARD premium.** Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. World Bank 'fertilizer prices >+30% 2026' forecast carries forward. India 5-nation tour Modi flies UAE May 15 — energy/fertilizer diversification optionality. Wheat +2.7% 1D / +15.0% 1M; Corn +3.2% 1D / +6.7% 1M continue strong but driven by Black Sea more than fertilizer pass-through today. PRIOR (May 13): Modi five-nation tour CONFIRMED May 15-20 (UAE-Netherlands-Sweden-Norway-Italy); UAE leg explicit focus on 'energy security for India amid disruptions in global supply chains due to US-Iran war' — DIRECT GOVERNMENT ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF FERTILIZER/IMPORT-COST BINDING. Iran FM Araghchi arrives New Delhi May 13 BRICS, meets Modi May 14 = Modi-Araghchi-UAE narrative bracket is MOST-ACTIONABLE NARRATIVE BRACKET OF CYCLE for fertilizer. India publicly admitting it cannot solve import-cost problem; Modi flying to UAE means Indian state's first-best solution is bilateral Gulf supply — exactly the situation that keeps CF/Nutrien/BASF pricing power. Qatar/Ras Laffan offline since Feb 28; QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailment structural; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. DAP India tender $935/t cfr May 11 = extraordinary print (pre-crisis $580-650); Urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% vs pre-war. CF YTD +37.7% 3M / +71% YTD (per UBS); UBS PT $97→$140; pricing-power capture confirmed in Q1 results ($615M earnings). **Corn +2.5% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through to grain producers visible.** Reversibility LOW: Qatar/Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60 days under any scenario. Cleanest medium-duration crisis-linked long. **Markets dossier E2: CF and Nutrien re-evaluated for first-class node promotion (CF already promoted May 12; Nutrien borderline — kept under monitor since 'second independent move' criterion not yet decisively met).** ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **CF INDUSTRIES +8.2% 1D TO $124.48 = MOVE OF THE DAY.** Sector regime shift: CF +8.2%, Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% all bid together = NOT a single-stock event. **CAUSAL CHAIN (~85% crisis-linked): (1) Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to CUT FERTILIZER USE ~50% (mixed organic) — one of seven 'consumption-restraint' appeals; market reads this as Indian-government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION that fertilizer-import shock is BINDING, not hypothetical. (2) Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28 attacks; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. (3) India's IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers at $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast — EXTRAORDINARY prints; DAP normally trades $580-650 ex-crisis. (4) Urea FOB US Gulf futures +50% since war began; UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly noting 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions.' (5) CF Q1 2026: net earnings $615M, EBITDA $983M confirm pricing-power capture; YTD shares now +71%.** Secondary contributor: Wheat +3.2% / Corn +3.2% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through to grain producers + tender activity. **This is a SUPPLY-DISRUPTION + DEMAND-CONFIRMATION confluence for the first time in cycle. REVERSIBILITY LOW: Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window even under ceasefire; QAFCO production scarring structural; India's import bill binding through monsoon planting season (June-Aug).** The fertilizer trade is the cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE: fertilizer-urea → india (existing edge, STRENGTHEN from weight ~5-6 to 7.5-8.0 on activation today via Modi address + DAP tender + US Gulf urea +50%).**

recent_signals.headline: Physical urea remains SOFT (~$397/t, -27% 1M) even as CF/NTR equity rose +2-3% on the risk-on day — the physical-disruption edge should NOT be strengthened on the equity move. A signed deal reopening Hormuz would further relieve the latent urea-export-transit risk (~40-49% of urea exports transit the strait).

Physical urea -1.1% to $397.50/t (-27.4% 1M); FOB Middle East ~$500 vs $512.50 prior. Physical DE-PRICING continues even as CF/NTR equity rallied +2% on crisis-rotation -- the physical-disruption edge should NOT be strengthened on the equity move. The Hormuz exposure (~40-49% of urea exports transit) is latent, not yet in the physical price.

**MIDDLE EAST UREA ~$416.50/T (-5.9% 1D) — THE FAST FERTILIZER LEG PRICES THE PEACE-DIVIDEND, FAR OFF THE ~$935-959/T INDIA PAID IN APRIL.** Middle East urea fell ~5.9% in a session to ~$416.50/T as the fertilizer leg prices the de-escalation/diplomacy track (oil down on diplomacy; the 5-6 June Gulf exchange was zero-casualty/calibrated). The war-premium that drove India's April DAP/urea tenders to ~$935-959/T cfr is bleeding out of the spot complex while the slow legs (insurance ~8x, freight, Qatar Ras Laffan scarring) lag. ~65-70% crisis (relief direction); symmetric and violent — the -5.9% reverses UP fast if talks break (cascade path 2: Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security). DISTINCT from the 5 June broad risk-off (gold -3.1%, oil -2%, VIX +39.7%), which was a RATES/AI move (Broadcom miss + hot May jobs), NOT a war risk-off.

recent_signals.headline: **FERTILIZER-EQUITY CONSOLIDATION OFF HIGHS vs PHYSICAL INTACT — CF -2.1% 1D / -5.3% MoM & NUTRIEN -4.4% MoM, BUT PHYSICAL UREA >$700/t (+40%, STRUCTURAL; NDSU SEES $850-900 PATH). PAPER UNWIND, PHYSICAL INTACT (C6).** (1) **The equity leg**: CF Industries -2.1% 1D / -5.3% MoM and Nutrien -4.4% MoM = fertilizer-EQUITY consolidation off highs on deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation (the US strikes May 25 went unanswered, intel A2) — the SAME paper crisis-trade unwind hitting tankers (FRO -3.0%), LNG (Cheniere -10.3% MoM), and defense (LMT -18.8% 3M). (2) **The physical leg is intact**: granular urea >$700/t (+40% from <$500 pre-war; NDSU sees an $850-900 path — the physical floor is RISING), TTF gas feedstock +30% YoY, Qatar Ras Laffan QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients still curtailed (~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected). Paper equities are SELLING the deal while the physical urea price prices ZERO operational change. (3) **C6 mechanism**: even on a clean signed deal the physical fertilizer cost stack (insurance NOT unwinding, shipping -36% volumes, gas feedstock) bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session. **Reversibility**: the equity component reverses on a deal break (an Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike, intel A3 — CF would snap +5-7%, urea re-bids); physical Qatar/Ras Laffan recovery is years out regardless. The investor read: the fertilizer longs that worked Feb-Apr have given back on paper; the physical premium is intact and structurally higher.

recent_signals.headline: **UREA (GRANULAR FOB ME) ~$502/t, -7.4% 1D — MODERATING ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION, BUT STILL ELEVATED vs ~$300/t PRE-WAR (~67% STRUCTURAL PREMIUM INTACT).** (1) **Mechanism**: urea fell -7.4% as the fertilizer leg of the deal-anticipation tape — US-Iran deal progress prices a partial Hormuz reopen, dumping the tail premium, co-moving with Brent (sub-$100), TTF gas (-4.2%), and India equities (+1.3%) in the LIVE clean-room session (US/UK markets closed). (2) **NOT a structural reset**: the physical fertilizer cost stack is intact — Qatar Ras Laffan QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients still curtailed, ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected, TTF gas feedstock still +25% YoY, DAP (FOB Egypt) ~$915-917/t (+35% cycle). Urea at ~$502 vs ~$300 pre-war is still a ~67% premium. (3) **C4 paper-physical non-confirmation**: paper prices the deal as near-done; physical (insurance 8x NOT unwinding, shipping volumes -36%, gas feedstock) prices ZERO operational change. Even on a clean signed deal the fertilizer cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session. (4) **CF Industries / Nutrien equities FROZEN** at Friday closes (US Memorial Day — NYSE did not settle; do NOT read a Monday move). **Reversibility**: paper component reverses on deal-failure / Israeli kinetic action (urea re-bids, CF would snap +5-7%); physical Qatar/Ras Laffan recovery is years out regardless of diplomatic resolution. (Staleness note: this corrects the prior May 19 lead, which framed a CF war-bid on a Brent-up day — today is the opposite, deal-relief leg, but the elevated-vs-pre-war floor is the durable read.)

recent_signals.headline: **WHEAT 678.75¢/BU +2.1% 1D / +13.7% 1M / +21.3% 3M — USDA PROJECTED 2026 ALL-WHEAT PRODUCTION 1,561M BUSHELS (-424M YoY) + HORMUZ FREIGHT BLEED.** Lower acreage + weaker yields per Fastmarkets/Trading Economics May 18 → wheat futures highest since June 2024. Black Sea winterkill story persists. Hormuz freight-risk premium bleeds into bulk grain through Persian Gulf / Red Sea lanes (secondary). **Rough rice 1,281.50¢/cwt +1.9% / +15.5% 1M**: NOT India supply story (India is at record stocks, easing export bans per UkrAgroConsult) → primary driver is the Hormuz freight premium bleed into all bulk grains. **Wheat ~35% crisis / 65% USDA supply shock**; Rice ~30% crisis. **Urea (Yuzhny FOB) ~$577/T +0.8% week; flat-to-up; -19% 1M = pre-cycle high mean-reversion. CF Industries -0.0% Tuesday (Nutrien +0.1%, BASF -0.9%).** Indian food-security cascade is the watch — wheat duty/export decisions out of Delhi over next 7 days will tell whether the government chooses to absorb or pass through.
